zh.lifeSummary: 當Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett於0957年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌蒙特爾賀利時，他的父親Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency32歲，母親Hildegarde de Blois11歲。他於1017年與Idelinde Chateau-Basset結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1012年之前在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-達瓦蒙特莫倫希。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bouchard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Michel, Normand, Pierre, Cecile, Gilles, Lucien, Alphonse, Jacques, Alain.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Burchard, composed of the elements burg ‘protection’ and hard ‘hard, strong’.

French: in some cases, possibly also a nickname for someone with a big mouth (in either a literal or figurative sense), from bouche ‘mouth’ + the pejorative suffix -ard. Compare Buschor , Bushard , Bushaw , and Bushor .

variants.name: Bushard
meanings: Americanized form of French   Bouchard  .
Possibly, in some cases, also an Americanized form of German Buschard (see Bushart 2).

variants.name: Buschor
meanings: Swiss German: probably a Germanized form of French   Bouchard  . Compare   Buschur   and   Bushor  .

variants.name: Bushore
meanings: Americanized form of French Bouchard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. Compare Bushor .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: 'le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

nameMeanings.name: Barbu'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: descriptive nickname from barbu ‘bearded’.
Romanian: from the old personal name Barbu of uncertain origin, usually thought to be a male derivative of the female name Barbura, an old vernacular form of Latin Barbara .

variants.name: Barbara
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

en.lifeSummary: When Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett was born in 0957, in Montlhéry, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, his father, Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency, was 32 and his mother, Hildegarde de Blois, was 11. He married Idelinde Chateau-Basset in 1017. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died before 1012, in Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett est né en 0957, à Montlhéry, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Hildegarde de Blois, avait 11 ans. Il a épousé Idelinde Chateau-Basset en 1017. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé avant 1012, à Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett 0957 in Montlhéry, Essonne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency 32 und seine Mutter Hildegarde de Blois 11. Er hat 1017 Idelinde Chateau-Basset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb vor 1012 in Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett è nato il nel 0957 nel Montlhéry, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Hildegarde de Blois, aveva 11 anni. Lui sposato Idelinde Chateau-Basset nel 1017. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì prima del 1012, nel Montmorency, Val-d'Oise, Île-de-France, Francia.

ko.lifeSummary: Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett(이)가 0957년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 에손 몽he리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Hildegarde de Blois(은)는 11세였다. 그는 1017년 Idelinde Chateau-Basset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1012년 이전 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-드'이스 몽모렌시에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett nasceu em 0957, em Montlhéry, Essonne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Hildegarde de Blois, tinha 11 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bouchard II 'le Barbu' de Montmorency, Seigneur d'Ecouen de Marlym si Feuillarde, de Château - Bassett родился в 0957 г. в местности монтлхeри, эссонн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Bouchard Bray-Sur-Seine De Montmorency было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Hildegarde de Blois, 11. Он женился на женщине по имени Idelinde Chateau-Basset в 1017 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер перед 1012 г. в местности монтморенси, вал-д'оис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.
